prompt:
Editor-core ticket. Our level editor's entity registry is a Vec<Entity> plus raw usize indices scattered across every subsystem, and it has burned us twice this quarter: delete an entity, the slot gets recycled by the next spawn, and some panel that cached the old index happily reads the NEW entity through the OLD number. Classic slot-reuse confusion, and it's unfindable in review because the code looks fine. I want the registry replaced with a generation-indexed slab arena so stale references are DETECTED instead of silently wrong.

This crate is rs-slabarena (lib name rs_slabarena, Cargo.toml already in place — leave it and .gitignore alone). Stdlib only, no unsafe needed. Everything in src/. The acceptance contract is tests/slabarena.rs, byte for byte as checked in: `cargo test --offline` fully green, test file untouched.

Public API, exactly this:

- `pub struct Handle` — opaque, `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]`, with accessors `pub fn index(&self) -> u32` and `pub fn generation(&self) -> u32`. index is the slot number; generation is how many times that slot has been freed before this tenancy.
- `pub struct Arena<T>` with `new()`, `Default`, `len() -> usize`, `is_empty() -> bool`, `capacity() -> usize`, `insert(&mut self, value: T) -> Handle`, `get(&self, h: Handle) -> Option<&T>`, `get_mut(&mut self, h: Handle) -> Option<&mut T>`, `remove(&mut self, h: Handle) -> Option<T>`, `contains(&self, h: Handle) -> bool`, and `iter(&self)` yielding `(Handle, &T)`.

Semantics I need pinned down (these are exactly what the tests check):

- Fresh slots number upward from 0 and start at generation 0. capacity() is the number of slots ever materialized (occupied + vacant); it never shrinks — removals leave a vacant slot behind for reuse, they don't compact.
- remove() hands back the value, frees the slot, and bumps that slot's generation, so every handle minted for the old tenancy is dead from that moment.
- Free slots are recycled most-recently-freed FIRST (LIFO free list — cache-warm slots first, and it makes reuse deterministic for the tests). A reused slot's new handle carries the bumped generation.
- Stale handle (freed, OR the slot has a new tenant at a later generation): get/get_mut/remove all return None, contains is false. A stale remove must be a no-op — under no circumstances may it evict the slot's current tenant. That's the whole point of the exercise.
- Iteration walks slot-index order, skips vacant slots, and yields the CURRENT handle (right generation) for each occupied slot. len() counts occupied slots only.
- T gets no bounds: no Clone, no Default, nothing. Values move in and move out.

Don't add public API beyond this — the registry wrapper lands in a follow-up. There's a scripted 600-op mixed-workload test in the contract that mirrors this exact spec (LIFO reuse, generation bumps, capacity accounting), so shortcuts will show up fast.
